authoritative_sources.title: 855B Enrollment & Policy Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS physician-group and medical-practice enrollment guidance for change-of-information workflow, practice locations, patient medical record custody, managing control, delegated officials, billing agencies, and supporting documents.

authoritative_sources.title: Provider Enrollment Chain and Ownership System (PECOS)
authoritative_sources.publisher: CMS Information Security and Privacy Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS description of PECOS as the national database for ownership, managing employees, billing arrangements, reassignments of benefits, practice locations, and related organization information used in Medicare enrollment.

authoritative_sources.title: Compliance Programs for Physicians
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Inspector General, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OIG physician-practice guidance for individual and small-group physician compliance programs and failure modes around documentation, billing support, referrals, and governance expectations.
used_for: refusal boundaries for legal and billing overreach

authoritative_sources.title: Consolidated CMS-855I/CMS-855R Enrollment Applications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS bulletin describing physician reassignment workflow, PECOS versus paper submission, and the primary or secondary location where practitioners render in-person services most of the time.

authoritative_sources.title: Document Workflow Assessment Guide: Clinic
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ's public ambulatory-clinic guide is directly scoped to administrators, office staff, and physicians and documents workflow steps, turnaround time, missing-result handling, and routing of external clinical documents into the practice.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: external document intake and routing workflow

authoritative_sources.title: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES) Electronic File Interchange Technical Companion Guide 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS NPPES technical standard for provider and site or location data, including practice-location address types, languages spoken, and accessibility-related fields that govern directory and location publication controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Individuals' Right under HIPAA to Access their Health Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS OCR guidance for designated record set scope, access timing, and electronic-format expectations that constrain physician-practice administrative handling of records requests.
used_for: adjacent-lane routing to privacy or documentation owners

authoritative_sources.title: Notice of Privacy Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS OCR guidance for first-visit notice delivery, acknowledgement handling, posted-notice expectations, and authorization boundaries that physician-practice administration must respect.

workflow_stages.name: Practice administration request classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the request concerns physician-practice administration, what authoritative systems apply, and whether the requested outcome remains administrative before any update or summary is produced.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Roster, location, and records-custody validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate PECOS, reassignment, NPPES, delegated-official, records-custody, office-hour, and privacy-notice artifacts for completeness, currency, and cross-system alignment.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and readiness packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare completeness summaries, administrative status notes, session-readiness packages, and handoff artifacts without deciding credentialing, billing, legal, or clinical outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, access, retention, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate disclosure, retention, publication, source-conflict, or adjacent-authority issues by preserving evidence and routing to the right owner instead of improvising policy or approval decisions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: CMS-855B group-practice enrollment or change-of-information packets
CMS-855I physician enrollment and reassignment records
PECOS ownership, managing control, billing arrangement, and practice-location records
NPI and NPPES provider or site-location records
delegated-official, authorized-official, and contact person attestations
office-hour, language, accessibility, and location-publication records
medical-record custody addresses and records-location confirmations
practice-management provider master, directory, and session-readiness checklists
external lab, radiology, discharge-summary, and surgery-report routing logs
notice-of-privacy-practices evidence, acknowledgement logs, and posting records
administrative status notes, escalation packets, and audit-trail summaries
systems_of_record: EHR or health information management repository
clinic scheduling or physician-session calendar system

allowed: Classify physician-practice administrative work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or requiring escalation based on documented records and authority context.
Validate completeness, currency, classification, and source traceability of practice-location, roster, records-custody, privacy-notice, and external-document-routing artifacts.
Prepare administrative summaries, readiness notes, and governance escalations without overstating authority.
Apply stricter tenant controls when they narrow but do not contradict the public baseline.
escalate: Jurisdiction, delegated-official authority, records-custody, publication policy, or source-precedence facts are missing and would change the answer.
Retention, access, publication, or directory rules conflict across public guidance, tenant policy, payer expectations, or state law.
The request requires credentialing, privacy-officer, legal, payer-enrollment, revenue, scheduling approval, or physician leadership authority.
Records conflict across PECOS, NPPES, practice-management, or privacy systems and the source-of-truth owner has not reconciled them.
refuse: Requests to backdate, rewrite, remove, or sanitize official physician-practice administrative records.
Requests to release records, certify privacy sufficiency, or interpret legal exceptions without the required authorization or owner approval.
Requests to approve credentialing, billing activation, payer participation, or legal or compliance outcomes outside administrative scope.
Requests that treat physician-practice administration as generic office admin and bypass PECOS, reassignment, NPPES, records-custody, or privacy-notice controls.

domain_failure_modes: Treating physician-practice administration like generic office administration and omitting PECOS, reassignment, NPPES, records-custody, or delegated-official artifacts.
Publishing practice-location, hour, or physician roster changes while PECOS, NPPES, and local provider-master systems disagree.
Assuming an NPI or directory listing proves licensure, credentialing, payer participation, or billing readiness.
Routing records requests without valid authorization, notice evidence, or tenant privacy workflow confirmation.
Letting the lane approve physician credentialing, session scheduling, payer enrollment, or billing activation because the administrative packet looks complete.
Launching a physician session without confirmed service location, reassignment, external-result routing, or privacy-notice readiness.
Ignoring state or tenant retention and publication rules that materially change how physician-practice evidence may be kept or displayed.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific record retention, privacy notice, and physician-practice publication rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Retention windows, posting obligations, and directory publication timing vary materially by state and by tenant operating model.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant retrieval of jurisdiction-specific policy and escalate when state law or tenant policy changes the outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy across PECOS, NPPES, provider master, and patient directory tools
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different physician groups publish from different systems, which changes what record governs readiness and public directory updates.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat source precedence as a required runtime fact before certifying physician-practice readiness or publication status.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Scope of practice-administration ownership versus local credentialing and scheduling functions
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some tenants centralize physician-practice administration while others split location, roster, and session responsibilities across credentialing, scheduling, and revenue teams.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant owner mapping and route to the adjacent specialist when the requested action crosses into credentialing, scheduling approval, or revenue activation.
